name: checkerboard
title: Checkerboard (negative surface tension)
question: Does a negative light-dark surface tension (gamma_ld=-3) drive intermixing into a checkerboard instead of sorting?
hypothesis: Cells maximize heterotypic contact, so the light-dark fractional interface dominates and remains high; defects persist by thermal activation.
objective: Simulate gamma_ld<0 from a random IC and measure fractional interface lengths, <n>, moments vs T (0-40) to characterize the checkerboard and its phase transitions.
description: Reproduction of the Fig 7+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=10, J_dd=8, J_ld=6, J_lM=12, J_dM=12 (gamma_ld=-3, gamma_lM=+7, gamma_dM=+8); T=10.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_random. Figures reproduced: Fig 7, Fig 8, Fig 9, Table I.
claim: gamma_ld<0 yields a defect-ridden checkerboard with dominant light-dark contact and two transitions (~T=0 freeze, ~T=15 mixing), matching Figs 7-9 and Table I.
expected_behavior: Light-dark heterotypic fraction is the largest cell-cell contact term

embed_visualizations.name: All paper figures
embed_visualizations.title: Checkerboard (negative surface tension) — full figure set

findings.statement: gamma_ld<0 yields a defect-ridden checkerboard with dominant light-dark contact and two transitions (~T=0 freeze, ~T=15 mixing), matching Figs 7-9 and Table I.

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Checkerboard (negative surface tension) example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 7, Fig 8, Fig 9, Table I.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

readouts.name: Total boundary length
readouts.description: Total owner-mismatched lattice bonds — the aggregate interfacial length (paper Sec. II D 1).

readouts.name: Fractional boundary lengths
readouts.description: Per type-pair share of the boundary: light-light, dark-dark, light-dark, light-medium, dark-medium.

readouts.name: Mean number of sides
readouts.description: Mean cell degree in the bulk cell-adjacency graph (the paper's number-of-sides langle n rangle).

readouts.name: Topological moments
readouts.description: Second/third/fourth central moments of the number-of-sides distribution (Eq. 6).

behavior_tests.name: Heterotypic contacts dominate (checkerboard mixing)
behavior_tests.description: Paper: Fig 7-8 — negative γ_ld drives a light/dark checkerboard. Pass criterion: light-dark fraction > 0.5 and larger than light-light & dark-dark. Measured from the reproduced run: frac_ld=0.837 vs frac_ll=0.042, frac_dd=0.061.
behavior_tests.pass_if: light-dark fraction > 0.5 and larger than light-light & dark-dark
behavior_tests.paper_reference: Fig 7-8 — negative γ_ld drives a light/dark checkerboard

derived.insight: gamma_ld<0 yields a defect-ridden checkerboard with dominant light-dark contact and two transitions (~T=0 freeze, ~T=15 mixing), matching Figs 7-9 and Table I.
